page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8y.large is an arm64-based virtual machine instance powered by the custom Alibaba YiTian710 processor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores running at 2.75 GHz with 1 thread per core, backed by 4.0 GB of system memory, which equates to 2.0 GB per core. The CPU architecture includes 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 64 MB L3 cache. It lacks local NVMe storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate moderate single-core capabilities but limited multi-core scaling. The single-core stress-ng score of 1814.78 and Geekbench single-core score of 1423.0 rank in the average tier. However, multi-core performance is weak, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 2555.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 1733.16. Memory bandwidth is mixed, showing a weak write speed of 15.07 GB/sec but an average uncached read speed of 39.5 GB/sec. Cryptographic performance is limited, as evidenced by a poor Passmark CPU encryption score of 323.78, despite supporting AES instructions.

From a resource density perspective, the instance offers a compute-oriented 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a cost-efficient entry point for arm64 development and testing without the overhead of larger instances. The primary tradeoff is the lack of local storage and hardware acceleration, alongside poor performance in complex vector or matrix operations due to the absence of AVX and AMX instruction sets.

The ecs.c8y.large is suited for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic web serving, text processing, and arm64 software compilation. It is also appropriate for running small-scale microservices, development environments, and low-intensity database operations that do not require high multi-core throughput or local SSD performance.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8y.large is an arm64-based instance powered by a 2.75 GHz Alibaba YiTian710 processor with 2 dedicated physical cores and 4.0 GB of RAM. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, this server features a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps without local storage. Benchmark data shows average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1423.0, but weak multi-core scaling and poor cryptographic execution. Lacking AVX and AMX extensions, it is not suited for heavy vector math or parallel processing. Qualitatively, it serves as a cost-efficient entry point for arm64 architecture validation. The instance is best suited for lightweight web serving, text processing, software compilation, and basic microservices.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8y.large is an arm64 server featuring a 2.75 GHz YiTian710 processor, 2 dedicated cores, and 4 GB RAM. It is designed for lightweight arm64 workloads and basic web serving.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8y.large is an arm64 instance with 2 dedicated YiTian710 cores, 4 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth for lightweight workloads.
tagline: A dedicated arm64 instance featuring a custom processor, tailored for lightweight single-threaded applications, software compilation, and basic web serving.
bullet_points: Powered by a custom 2.75 GHz Alibaba YiTian710 arm64 processor
Features 2 dedicated physical cores and 4.0 GB of system memory
Provides 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with network-attached storage
Delivers average single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling
Best suited for lightweight web serving, text processing, and arm64 development
